headline: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 and Mythos family release dominated the quarter while orchestration techniques rotated down.
body: Anthropic shipped the Mythos model family alongside Fable 5, with Fable 5 positioned as Mythos 5 with guardrails and offered at no extra cost on Claude Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise plans through June 22 before reverting to usage credits [4][7]. Demonstrations spanned autonomous Factorio play, a physics-derived solar system simulation predicting eclipses, a fluid simulation synced to a Beethoven remix, and a self-built CAD editor with an AI copilot, all foregrounding ai-agents and code generation [8][9][10][11]. Meanwhile the orchestration cluster, agent-orchestration, prompt-chaining, ai-layer, and context-management, weakened together, and infrastructure techniques broke out, including auto-scaling and fully-sharded-data-parallelism, with RunPod pitching decorator-based GPU deployment and serverless autoscaling from the IDE [5]. Workflow-automation improved while memory-systems and percolation registered as fresh outliers.

cited_items.title: Claude Fable + Mythos 5 Are Live & The Numbers are INSANE
GPU Cloud Deployment Without Leaving Your IDE — Audry Hsu, RunPod
